167433.explanation: Although it is an option in exceptional cases, a referendum may be available. Under normal circumstances, the municipal council as representatives of the people will consider the various opinions and viewpoints in their deliberations.

167437.explanation: The obligation can be debated, but CDA Westerveld values the accessibility of administrators.

167438.explanation: Although lowering the property tax (ozb) seems like a popular move, as a municipality we cannot reduce it without countermeasures on expenditures. At the moment, sharp choices must already be made to spend the available budget wisely.

167439.explanation: In the (aging) municipality of Westerveld, there is a great need for practically skilled people in garden and construction maintenance. However, the municipality does not need to actively influence this. The small-scale businesses develop themselves according to needs and possibilities.

167440.explanation: The current industrial estates are full. 'More' and/or 'expansion' of existing industrial estates stimulate the establishment of new or growing companies.

167442.explanation: Cooperation can be financially attractive, but it is often a local initiative. The municipality cannot financially support every project. Possibly a contribution can be made from provincial subsidies.

167443.explanation: A resident may receive permission for small-scale solar-on-land for personal use. Large-scale (commercial) solar-on-land is currently not permitted in Westerveld. CDA Westerveld wants to wait for the already approved solar-on-water projects before considering solar-on-land.

167445.explanation: We respect each other's administrative responsibility.

167446.explanation: CDA Westerveld is satisfied with the current organic and sustainable considerations in mowing policy. This already results in less frequent mowing. No additional measures are necessary.

167448.explanation: Waste processing costs money. Those who bring a lot of waste currently also contribute financially with a small fee.

167450.explanation: CDA Westerveld believes we may enjoy and recreate in all our nature areas.

167452.explanation: CDA Westerveld is pleased with the positive municipal approach to the reception of refugees and status holders. The faster integration occurs, the better the connection in the neighborhood and society. This may cost more money.

167454.explanation: It is not about the number. We must simply make our contribution to the national and Drenthe targets.

167456.explanation: If it concerns a temporary problem, CDA Westerveld wants to (financially) help. If there is a long-term major problem, the association must also be realistic about the future.

167459.explanation: CDA Westerveld currently sees no reason to appoint additional enforcement officers (BOAs).

167462.explanation: There are some through (school) bicycle routes that may be reconsidered, but otherwise it is precisely a municipal strength to have darkness.

167463.explanation: The use of crop protection or pest control products falls within an entrepreneur’s business operations. We should not financially interfere with that. If the municipality imposes it by regulations, they must compensate for it.

167464.explanation: We would like more social rental housing, but in our villages there is more need for specific affordable elderly and/or small family homes, which do not necessarily have to be social rental housing.

167465.explanation: High-rise residential buildings do not fit in the villages of a rural municipality like Westerveld. Two floors above a shopping layer or multifunctional location is already high for a village.

167466.explanation: In special situations, a holiday home can serve as a permanent address for a short period. In the long term, a holiday home remains intended for recreation. For short periods, the municipality can grant temporary exemptions to meet a need.

167470.explanation: We see that the demand for care will increase due to the larger number of elderly people in our municipality. We must provide care homes combined with strengthening the self-reliant community.

167472.explanation: Where possible, we apply wider income limits in our municipality so that specific support is possible.

167473.explanation: "In exchange for" is not the right term, but it helps people participate in an active community. Growth in social contacts reduces loneliness and promotes neighborliness (noaberschap) in society.

167474.explanation: As a municipality, we should look after people who are struggling. In some cases, we may forgive taxes. That helps reduce financial worries.

167475.explanation: We must follow the legal rules on additional earnings. There may be some room for extra gifts from family.

167479.explanation: This is already largely the case in the main villages. In rural areas, some parts within the built-up area still have 50 km/h speed limits.

167480.explanation: The many cycling recreationists and schoolchildren ask for respect from motorists. This often works well in the villages.

167483.explanation: Heavy work traffic already chooses the most appropriate route around the villages, but there are a few (provincial) connecting roads through the village centers.

167485.explanation: Clear information from our movement coach helps residents of Westerveld enjoy a healthy old age.

167486.explanation: The most important thing is that appropriate care is offered based on the help request. The bill comes second. Any financial shortfalls must be supplemented.

167487.explanation: CDA Westerveld expects a library function in every provision village and advocates supportive offerings in other villages, such as in community centers or via the mobile library.

167433.explanation: VVD Westerveld supports a structured participation approach appropriate to scale and impact. Caveat: VVD Westerveld finds referenda unsuitable for complex decision-making: all nuances are reduced to a simple yes/no. That does not fit within a representative democracy.

167437.explanation: VVD Westerveld endorses accessible and regular contact moments between aldermen and residents as a crucial step toward more transparency and citizen involvement.

167438.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants to lower the property tax (ozb) to financially relieve residents and compel the municipality toward efficient and responsible policies. The government should not collect unnecessary money but give space to citizens and businesses.

167439.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants to invest in promotion to attract companies that create jobs for practically trained residents, as part of a broader vision on economic growth and inclusive employment.

167440.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants more industrial estates in the municipality and expansion of existing ones. This provides space for companies to settle, leading to more jobs and a stronger local economy. The municipality must actively make plans for this but consider the surroundings.

167442.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants the municipality to actively support citizens who want to produce sustainable energy themselves, especially through small-scale projects, so that citizen participation and sustainability go hand in hand. The municipality is not the financier of local energy initiatives.

167443.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants to allow solar parks on agricultural land provided the application is multifunctional (water storage, grazing, and energy generation). Additionally, focus on sustainable energy via roofs and buildings, but not wind turbines.

167445.explanation: VVD Westerveld is positive about small-scale, locally appropriate energy generation but not about large-scale wind farms, especially not near and certainly not within the municipality borders.

167446.explanation: VVD Westerveld emphasizes that this requires tailor-made approaches and that other interests such as safety, cleanliness, and local initiatives must be maintained. VVD Westerveld is not opposed to ecological mowing management, provided these conditions are met.

167448.explanation: VVD Westerveld is not generally in favor of free waste disposal unless there is clear financial coverage and environmental benefit. Making waste disposal completely free can lead to higher municipal costs, which does not align with striving for healthy finances.

167450.explanation: VVD Westerveld wants to spread and make tourism and recreation accessible so that residents and visitors can enjoy nature. This idea fits with our goal of an attractive living environment. Westerveld must not become a nature museum.

167452.explanation: VVD Westerveld supports humane crisis and regular reception of asylum seekers. Every municipality should contribute. We find Westerveld's current efforts appropriate.

167454.explanation: VVD Westerveld believes there is already a legal obligation, which is being met, to provide reception, but sees no need for two permanent asylum seeker centers. We advocate for small-scale or temporary solutions rather than large, permanent centers.

167456.explanation: VVD Westerveld holds that associations are primarily responsible for their own finances. The municipality can assist with facilities or temporary support in exceptional cases but is not obligated to do so. No structural subsidies, but recognition of the importance of sports in society.

167459.explanation: VVD Westerveld supports more enforcement and preventive presence. This means better control of disturbances and compliance. Additionally, the public order officials (boa's) relieve the police from local tasks. The extra boa's must fit within a responsible budget.

167462.explanation: VVD Westerveld considers safety important and therefore supports lighting at dangerous locations or routes. Due to cost awareness, this cannot be everywhere, but only where truly needed. We must also consider a balance with nature to avoid excessive light pollution.

167463.explanation: VVD Westerveld believes the municipality should not provide structural subsidies. The responsibility lies with the market or the national government. Sustainability is important, but stimulation should be through knowledge, permits, and cooperation, not municipal payments.

167464.explanation: VVD Westerveld finds a fixed percentage inappropriate and advocates building according to local needs. It’s about balance: enough social housing for affordability and flow-through, room for owner-occupied and mid-rent homes. Social housing yields less return, which can lead to project cancellations.

167465.explanation: VVD Westerveld is against buildings higher than four floors. This protects the open, green landscape and prevents urbanization. However, we must continuously balance rural identity against efficient use of scarce space.

167466.explanation: VVD Westerveld is not in favor of permanent residence in holiday parks. This reduces recreational facilities availability. Furthermore, permanent residence requires better roads, sewage, and infrastructure.

167470.explanation: VVD Westerveld deems it important that there are sufficient suitable homes so that people in need of care do not unnecessarily end up in expensive care institutions and elderly can move to appropriate housing. It's a trade-off between financial burdens and social benefits.

167472.explanation: VVD Westerveld finds the current threshold appropriate. However, we must prevent people just above the limit from falling between the cracks. This means avoiding situations where workers or low-income individuals receive no assistance despite struggling to make ends meet.

167473.explanation: VVD Westerveld believes welfare is a safety net, not a hammock. Welfare is intended as temporary assistance and recipients should give something back to the community. It’s about social activation and preventing passivity and social isolation.

167474.explanation: VVD Westerveld believes everyone should contribute to municipal provisions. Remission is unfair. We hold that the principle of equal treatment is more important than income-dependent exemptions. Taxes are a contribution everyone must make.

167475.explanation: Currently there are strict rules: additional earnings are often offset against the benefit. VVD wants to relax these rules so people can keep extra income without immediate full deduction of benefits. This encourages labor participation and self-reliance.

167479.explanation: Lower speed reduces the risk of serious accidents, especially for pedestrians and cyclists, and causes less noise nuisance and a safer feeling in residential neighborhoods. However, it must not come at the expense of traffic flow.

167480.explanation: VVD Westerveld finds it unnecessarily restrictive for motorists and traffic flow. There are already sufficient rules to protect cyclists. We do support looking into fast and safe cycling routes.

167483.explanation: VVD Westerveld supports efficient agricultural traffic but also safety and livability. Our preference is for pragmatic solutions like detours and improved infrastructure, not a total ban.

167485.explanation: VVD Westerveld sees health as a personal responsibility of residents. It is not something the municipality should spend money or resources on. The government should not unnecessarily take on tasks citizens can perform themselves and should focus on its core tasks.

167486.explanation: VVD Westerveld believes the national government is responsible for sufficient funding and that municipalities should not structurally top up, as this comes at the expense of other facilities. It’s important to work efficiently and improve cooperation between healthcare parties and government.

167487.explanation: Additional municipal spending on book lending in every village is unnecessary because a library already provides the basic service. The municipality should not unnecessarily expand in areas already adequately arranged within existing structures.

167433.explanation: Residents should be able to participate in important decisions. This can also be done via a referendum. However, we believe this should only happen under clear conditions to ensure it is careful, fair, and well-prepared.

167437.explanation: Aldermen, council members, and officials must be visible and approachable for residents. Aldermen can do this by regularly holding consultation hours in the different villages so residents can easily make contact.

167438.explanation: We believe those with the greatest means should bear the greatest burdens. With the property tax (ozb) we collectively pay for amenities like healthcare, sports, and culture. In Westerveld, the ozb is already lower than average. Lowering it means cutting back on facilities.

167439.explanation: We want valuable work in a fair economy. That is an economy that is sustainable and circular. This requires practically and theoretically educated people with jobs that fit Westerveld.

167440.explanation: Current business parks can be better utilized. Larger companies outside business parks often cause a lot of heavy traffic, which harms livability. This calls for better choices in company locations.

167442.explanation: Residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy deserve support from the municipality. Energy cooperatives ensure that revenues remain local and more people can participate in the energy transition.

167443.explanation: We do not want to use agricultural land for solar panels. This land is needed for extensive, sustainable, and organic farming. We place solar panels mainly on roofs, along roads, and on industrial sites.

167445.explanation: We want clean energy. Westerveld should determine together with neighboring municipalities how wind energy can be possible in the region. Attention must be given to careful adjustments, distance to residences, and wind turbine height.

167446.explanation: Less frequent mowing of verges is good for bees, insects, and biodiversity. We consider traffic safety and accessibility. Verges are a supplement but do not compensate for biodiversity loss in the more than 30,000 hectares of nature in Westerveld.

167448.explanation: We want a circular economy with less waste. By not keeping certain waste streams free, we encourage waste separation and reuse. This is better for the environment and keeps costs fair.

167450.explanation: The beautiful nature of Westerveld is our calling card and must be accessible to residents and tourists via walking and cycling paths. Animals also need space, for example birds during the breeding season. Therefore, we want core and quiet areas to be inaccessible to people.

167452.explanation: We want a society where everyone can participate. Investing in integration helps newcomers find their place faster and strengthens cohesion in villages and neighborhoods. Investing in language, education, participation, and socio-economic opportunities is essential for successful integration.

167454.explanation: We see refugee reception as a humanitarian duty. People fleeing war or violence must be safely received. Therefore, we want two small-scale, permanent locations with a maximum of 150 people each. This provides peace and facilitates integration through school, sports, and work.

167456.explanation: The municipality contributes ideas for solutions for sports clubs. Financial aid is not always the right solution. Sometimes collaboration or other forms of support are better for the future.

167459.explanation: Extra deployment and visibility of public order officers (boa’s) is needed, especially now that there is less police presence. This allows better enforcement of rules and makes residents feel safer.

167462.explanation: We must counter light pollution. Darkness in rural areas is important for nature and landscape. We want only heavily used routes and intersections to be lit in a targeted and innovative way where safety demands it.

167463.explanation: Farmers should receive appropriate compensation for what they do, such as placing hedges and transitioning to organic and nature-inclusive farming, not for maintaining harmful practices.

167464.explanation: The share of social rental housing in Westerveld is currently about 16%. This is too low. By ensuring at least 33% social rental housing in new developments, we provide more affordable homes for starters and people with lower incomes.

167465.explanation: Higher buildings do not fit the character of Westerveld. High-rise can only be allowed in villages or when renovating existing tall buildings if it is well integrated into the surroundings.

167466.explanation: The existing Vital Holiday Parks policy remains leading. On parks that are not vital, permanent residence could be allowed under conditions for residents who otherwise have no options.

167470.explanation: The municipality does not invest directly in residential complexes. However, the municipality is responsible for providing enough housing for all resident groups, including those who need care. We manage this through agreements with partners.

167472.explanation: We want to support residents in poverty as well as possible. The municipality already applies high limits; in special situations deviations must be possible. This way, people with just too little income can receive help and bigger problems can be prevented.

167473.explanation: Social assistance is a right. People who can and want to work, we encourage and guide towards meaningful and suitable jobs. Mandatory work as a condition does not fit and does not take individual circumstances into account.

167474.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes must remain possible when the situation requires it. This prevents problems from becoming bigger.

167475.explanation: Currently, you can keep part of your additional earnings for the first 6 months on social assistance. We want to increase this to 30% and extend the period, as is done in other municipalities. This encourages work, increases the chance of a permanent job, and raises income.

167479.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit increases traffic safety. Road design must be adapted accordingly so that traffic naturally slows down. This allows children to go to school safely and independently and makes pedestrians and cyclists feel safer.

167480.explanation: By giving cyclists priority more often and designing roads accordingly, we make traffic safer and encourage sustainable transport. We choose the STOMP principle: first walking, then cycling, public transport, and only then the car.

167483.explanation: Agricultural traffic can cause unsafe situations in villages. Where possible, we look for solutions outside the villages, such as alternative routes or traffic measures, so that villages remain safe and livable.

167485.explanation: The municipality can contribute to a healthier lifestyle. It can pay more attention to healthy eating and limit the availability of unhealthy food. Sports and exercise coaches also play an important role in encouraging healthy behavior.

167486.explanation: The national government sets the rules for youth care, but the municipality has to pay. That is not right. The municipality must regain control over youth care. Youth assistance should be low-threshold and quickly available. By detecting problems earlier, heavier and longer-term care is less often needed.

167487.explanation: Borrowing books in your own village is important for social interaction, enjoyment of reading, and development. By investing in libraries and lending points, we ensure that knowledge and culture remain accessible to everyone. We arrange home delivery of books for anyone who cannot physically visit the library.

167433.explanation: Referendums should be used only on subjects that residents indicate are important. We believe that preserving the swimming pools is such a topic.

167437.explanation: If aldermen themselves indicate they want a consultation hour, we are supportive. However, we do not consider it an obligation.

167438.explanation: If we as Westerveld residents want to maintain facilities at a good level, it may be necessary to increase the property tax to afford all desired services.

167439.explanation: There is currently enough work for those with practical training, but we suspect this will change in the (near) future due to stricter environmental regulations, fewer financing options for businesses, and the influx of practically trained newcomers.

167440.explanation: Existing industrial areas must be able to expand if needed, but no new industrial areas should be created.

167442.explanation: The municipality considers which sustainable energy projects require financing and which do not.

167443.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land are possible if there is a dual function. Examples of such a dual function are solar panels combined with livestock, arable farming, and/or water storage.

167445.explanation: We do not want new wind turbines on our municipality border. More research must first be done on the impact of wind turbines on the health of people and animals.

167446.explanation: Less mowing increases biodiversity, which is better for nature in Westerveld.

167448.explanation: Free waste disposal in Havelte encourages proper sorting and conscious waste management. Waste can then be reused as raw materials.

167450.explanation: We want to make as many nature areas as possible accessible to walkers, but not all. Not in areas where walkers could disturb the nature.

167452.explanation: By investing money in integration, the municipality ensures that people can build a dignified life, participate in society, and adopt the norms and values of Westerveld.

167454.explanation: Permanent asylum seeker centers offer advantages such as permanent jobs for Westerveld residents, faster integration, and labor from the center. Having fixed shelter locations also means residents know what to expect.

167456.explanation: The municipality must have the option to financially support sports clubs. However, the municipality should be able to make a considered assessment.

167459.explanation: For a large area like Westerveld, two municipal enforcement officers are too few. More enforcement officers means greater visibility on the streets, more attention to livability, and increases the feeling of safety.

167462.explanation: In some rural areas, darkness is preferable, but in places where many people pass by, more lighting is desirable.

167463.explanation: Plant protection products are used, some containing toxic components and others not. Artificial fertilizer must be used because less organic manure is allowed. It is therefore difficult to determine when the municipality should or should not contribute financially.

167464.explanation: We need more social rental housing for our own youth and the elderly. Currently, there is insufficient flow and too little chance of finding affordable housing. The percentage of social housing in Westerveld may be higher than the national percentage.

167465.explanation: If a taller residential building can fit within the landscape or village, it can provide desirable, cheaper housing units. There must be sufficient support from local residents. We consider this support more important than the opinion of the Aesthetics Committee.

167466.explanation: If holiday homes meet the residential standards for permanent residence, they should be allowed as permanent addresses. This creates extra housing without new construction and increases opportunities for moving within the housing market.

167470.explanation: Investing in residential complexes with care can realize plans that have been in preparation for years but have not taken off. We believe there is a great need for this, especially among the aging Westerveld population. We also support rebuilding nursing homes.

167472.explanation: Some Westerveld residents cannot make ends meet on 130% of a social assistance benefit, the current standard. We believe these residents should also be helped, which can be done by raising the income limit.

167473.explanation: Meaningful work alongside a social assistance benefit is possible. This must be tailored and done in consultation. Coercion does not motivate. We do believe more people can be useful to society.

167474.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes can prevent greater financial problems and thus social problems. We prefer waiving taxes over people feeling forced to steal.

167475.explanation: More opportunities for additional earnings give Westerveld residents on social assistance the chance to independently leave the benefit system.

167479.explanation: We think the maximum speed limits are well arranged in Westerveld: 30 km/h where necessary, 50 km/h where possible.

167480.explanation: We do not believe that giving cyclists priority more often will result in greater road safety, unless more traffic lights are installed. This brings additional costs. The question is whether these costs are justified in relation to the goal.

167483.explanation: Tractors are road users and are allowed to drive where permitted, including in village centers.

167485.explanation: More education about healthy living is good, although we think the municipality is already doing a lot in this area. Think of funding for movement coaches and for working groups of involved Westerveld residents who provide information about healthy living.

167486.explanation: For us, the well-being of the individual comes first. If there is insufficient funding for youth care from the national government, the municipality must supplement it.

167487.explanation: We believe borrowing books is already well organized with libraries in the larger villages and mini libraries elsewhere. If there is more demand, this can be discussed.

parties.name: DSW/GBW
parties.fullName: Duurzaam Sterk Westerveld & Gemeente Belangen Westerveld

167433.explanation: We do not support a referendum. In elections, residents choose their own representatives. However, decisions can be so important that you want to give residents extra influence by involving them early in plans that affect them. Residents can also always approach us in between to share their views.

167437.explanation: We are not in favor of a weekly mandatory consultation hour. Aldermen are there for our residents and must be approachable. Residents can make an appointment for this purpose. We also expect aldermen to have an open attitude and to actively seek dialogue with society.

167438.explanation: We believe all taxes should remain as low as possible. Because municipalities are increasingly getting 'tasks without money' from the government, finances are tight. Therefore, lowering the property tax (ozb) is not possible.

167439.explanation: Through the Zwolle Region, we want to actively focus on creating new jobs in Westerveld. It is important that young people can continue living here and find enjoyable work, for example in healthcare, technology, defense, agriculture, education, or specialized companies.

167440.explanation: Yes, if the existing industrial areas are fully built-up. Industrial areas are intended for Westerveld companies that are outgrowing their current space. Existing industrial areas must be able to grow with demand.

167442.explanation: We definitely want to continue encouraging the generation of sustainable energy, for example through small-scale energy cooperatives.

167443.explanation: National legislation prohibits placing solar panels on land zoned for agriculture. Therefore, this is not possible in Westerveld.

167445.explanation: We are not in favor of large-scale wind turbine parks in or on the outskirts of Westerveld. If wind turbines are placed in neighboring municipalities, we want the sightlines in Westerveld to be disturbed as little as possible.

167446.explanation: We want an attractive and safe public space. Sustainability and biodiversity are part of the policy. When deciding whether or not to mow, we want local residents to have a say.

167448.explanation: The principle that 'the polluter pays' results in a fair distribution of disposal costs at the waste collection point. Residents pay by weight for what they bring in. This is cost-covering.

167450.explanation: Nature reserves must remain freely accessible to residents and tourists. People should be able to enjoy our nature. This also creates support for maintenance, costs, and preservation. Temporary measures for vulnerable species, such as breeding cranes, must be possible.

167452.explanation: There is already enough money for shelter, integration, and participation in society. The Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers and the municipality cooperate on this. Many volunteers also help, for example, as language assistants or companions.

167454.explanation: We believe that we already offer sufficient small-scale shelter and do not see the necessity to accommodate larger asylum seeker centers (azc). Our municipality is best suited for small-scale shelter.

167456.explanation: Sports are important and we want to maintain them. Associations also have their own responsibility. In cases of problems and changed legislation, the municipality must consider the viability of associations and sometimes offer a helping hand.

167459.explanation: We have not received signals that the enforcement officers (boa's) cannot handle their work. If increasing problems make it necessary, we will assess based on complaints or incidents whether more funding is needed. We do see that extra effort in tackling undermining can yield a lot.

167462.explanation: If safety is at risk, streetlights can provide improvement. An example is the lighting from Havelte to Meppel along the dark section by the canal. Darkness is not inherently unsafe and also provides peace for animals.

167463.explanation: Farmers are entrepreneurs and must comply with legal frameworks and their own business operations. It is not a municipal task to provide compensation in this context.

167464.explanation: For the largest housing association in Westerveld, 33% social rental housing is financially and organizationally unfeasible. We would like to explore what a second cooperative can contribute in this regard.

167465.explanation: We find that more than four stories is not appropriate in Westerveld. Multi-story housing can help address housing shortages. Buildings must fit the surroundings. We prefer not to have block-shaped buildings in our municipality.

167466.explanation: The minister requires this in certain cases. Nevertheless, in Westerveld we want to preserve our holiday parks for recreation. Where rights have been established for permanent residence, we would like zoning within the park so that living and recreation are separated.

167470.explanation: It is important that people who need care can continue living in the municipality. The municipality must support initiatives, for example through agreements with housing corporations. Care housing can never be set up by the municipality alone. This requires cooperation with care organizations.

167472.explanation: We believe the current income limit of 120% of the minimum wage as a threshold for assistance should remain. This works well and ensures dignified conditions, combined with the generous minimum policy. With this standard, our social policy leads the way in the Netherlands.

167473.explanation: A counter-performance is requested but not always possible. We do not want people receiving social assistance to be stigmatized. Dignity is important.

167474.explanation: No, because this remission is very important for people with a low income. We want to maintain it.

167475.explanation: We see this as a good step towards full participation in society.

167479.explanation: Traffic in the villages must be safe and suited to the local situation. Clarity and visibility are especially important.

167480.explanation: Cyclists are vulnerable road users and should primarily be able to cycle safely. We find clarity, visibility, and lighting more important than priority.

167483.explanation: Tractors belong to our rural municipality. In some places, large agricultural vehicles can cause unsafe situations. This requires a tailored approach and problem areas must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

167485.explanation: Health and well-being are important for our residents. Therefore, we see the added value of movement coaches. They encourage exercise and a healthy lifestyle. This must be maintained.

167486.explanation: First and foremost, young people must always receive the help they need. If the national contribution is insufficient, this must be paid from the municipality's own resources. Young people have the right to a good foundation.

167487.explanation: A library is an important facility that does more than just lend books. Think of help with illiteracy and computer skills. This helps people to (continue to) participate in society. We must preserve the current libraries and keep them accessible to everyone. Additional libraries are not necessary.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Tractors may not drive through village centres unless that is the only route.
The municipality must invest money in a promotion campaign to attract companies that employ many workers with practical/vocational skills.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: om bedrijven aan te trekken waar veel praktisch geschoolden werken.

statements.title: The municipality must mow roadside verges and grass fields less often so nature has more space.
The municipality must invest money in building residential complexes for people who need care.
More business parks must be developed in Westerveld.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The residents of Westerveld must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Westerveld moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Two permanent asylum seeker centres (AZCs) must be established in our municipality.

titleParts.text: In onze gemeente moeten twee permanente

titleParts.glossary: Reception centers for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers' center remains in a municipality for a longer period.

statements.title: Dropping off all types of waste at the Havelte waste transfer station must be free.
Westerveld must urge neighbouring municipalities not to place new wind turbines near the municipal border.

titleParts.text: Westerveld moet er bij aangrenzende gemeenten op aandringen dat zij geen nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: At least 33% of newly built homes in Westerveld must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Westerveld moet minimaal 33% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: No buildings of more than four storeys may be built in Westerveld.
All nature areas in Westerveld must be accessible to walkers.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should provide residents with more information about healthy living.
In Westerveld there should be four municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). Currently there are two.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen. Nu zijn dat er twee.

statements.title: Cyclists should be given priority over motorists more often.
Every alderman should hold a weekly consultation hour for residents.

titleParts.glossary: A fixed time when residents can ask questions or drop in.

statements.title: The municipality should install more streetlights in the countryside.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer lantaarnpalen in het

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more on youth care than it receives from the national government.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

titleParts.text: dan wat ze daarvoor krijgt van het Rijk.

statements.title: If farmers do not use crop protection products and artificial fertilizers, the municipality should provide them with financial support for that.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should, with extra funding, ensure residents can borrow books in their own villages.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.

shootoutStatements.title: Residents receiving social assistance should have more opportunities to earn extra income.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.
The municipality should support more people in poverty by raising the income threshold for assistance.
